prompt:
Data-platform ticket. Our log-enrichment pipeline joins events against a warehouse SKU lookup that someone re-uploads into Datadog by hand through the UI every Monday — twice now the upload silently dropped rows and nobody noticed until dashboards went weird mid-week. Build a Java client class `ReferenceTablesClient` (stdlib only; a small Json helper ships in Json.java — use it) that manages Datadog Reference Tables through the CURRENT v2 API. TestMain.java is the acceptance contract (run: java TestMain.java).

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the current official Datadog material for the v2 reference-tables interface — the API reference and/or the official datadog-api-client-java sources on GitHub (DataDog org), which count as first-party. Nail down the documented multi-step upload flow for local-file tables (what you POST first, what comes back, where the CSV bytes actually go, and how the table creation references the upload), the create-table schema and primary-key rules including the allowed field types, how table processing is observed and what error details a failed table carries, the row-replacement operation and what must NOT be resent on it, and the list operation's offset pagination controls. Blogs and Stack Overflow do not count as sources. Our pinned wire contract lives in docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected fixtures. Compare the live docs against them — if anything disagrees, report the conflict explicitly in your summary and leave the fixtures and tests untouched. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

What the tests use (see docs/contract.json for pinned shapes):
- new ReferenceTablesClient(String baseUrl, String apiKey, String appKey, java.util.function.LongConsumer sleeper) — both documented auth headers on every Datadog request, never in the query string, and NEVER forwarded to the presigned part-upload URLs; sleeper is the only way to wait (no Thread.sleep).
- static planUploadParts(long totalBytes) -> UploadPlan record (partCount(), partSize()) implementing the documented part limits; rejects empty and over-limit files with IllegalArgumentException.
- createTable(tableName, description, List<String> tags, LinkedHashMap<String,String> fields, String primaryKey, String csv) -> table id: validates the schema against the documented field types and the single-primary-key rule BEFORE any request (IllegalArgumentException naming the problem), then walks the documented upload flow and creates the table with source LOCAL_FILE, schema fields in the given order, the primary key, and the upload reference.
- waitUntilReady(tableId, pollIntervalMillis, maxPolls) -> TableStatus record (status(), rowCount()): polls the documented read operation, sleeping pollIntervalMillis between polls only, at most maxPolls polls; a terminal failure raises TableProcessingException exposing errorMessage(), errorRowCount(), errorType() from the documented error fields; still-processing after maxPolls raises IllegalStateException.
- replaceRows(tableId, tableName, List<String> headers, String csv): fresh upload, then the documented row-replacement operation referencing the NEW upload id — without resending schema or source.
- listTables(int pageLimit) -> List<TableSummary> (record: id(), tableName(), status(), rowCount()): walks the documented offset pagination from the start, advancing by pageLimit until a short page, preserving server order.
- DatadogApiException (statusCode(), errors()) decoded from the documented error envelope, message carrying status and detail but never credentials.

Acceptance: java TestMain.java — it runs a loopback fake Datadog API; no real Datadog, no real credentials. TestMain.java and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them. Json.java is starter code you may extend but the tests don't require changes to it.

official_sources.title: datadog-api-client-java v2 ReferenceTablesApi.java (official Datadog client source)
official_sources.used_for: the full operation set: POST /api/v2/reference-tables/uploads (201) then PUT CSV chunks to each returned URL then POST /api/v2/reference-tables/tables (201) with upload_id in file_metadata; GET /api/v2/reference-tables/tables/{id}; PATCH /api/v2/reference-tables/tables/{id} for data updates (source type cannot change after creation); GET /api/v2/reference-tables/tables with page[limit] (default 15), page[offset] (default 0), sort (default -updated_at) and filter params; auth via apiKeyAuth and appKeyAuth

official_sources.title: datadog-api-client-java CreateTableRequestDataAttributes.java (official Datadog client source)
official_sources.used_for: create-table attributes: table_name, schema, and source are required; description, tags, and file_metadata are optional

official_sources.title: datadog-api-client-java CreateTableRequestDataAttributesSchema.java (official Datadog client source)
official_sources.used_for: schema shape: required fields array and required primary_keys array; only one primary key is supported and it is used as an ID to retrieve rows

official_sources.title: datadog-api-client-java ReferenceTableSchemaFieldType.java (official Datadog client source)
official_sources.used_for: the only documented schema field types: STRING and INT32

official_sources.title: datadog-api-client-java CreateUploadRequestDataAttributes.java (official Datadog client source)
official_sources.used_for: upload request attributes: headers (CSV columns in file order), table_name, part_count (maximum 20), part_size (all parts except the last at least 5,000,000 bytes)

official_sources.title: datadog-api-client-java CreateUploadResponseDataAttributes.java (official Datadog client source)
official_sources.used_for: upload response: part_urls presigned URLs for PUTting parts, expiring after 5 minutes

official_sources.title: datadog-api-client-java TableResultV2DataAttributes.java (official Datadog client source)
official_sources.used_for: table result attributes: status (processing status), row_count (successfully processed rows), schema, source, and file_metadata error reporting (error_message, error_row_count, error_type present only when errors occur)

research.language_rationale: The data-platform services that enrich logs with warehouse lookups are Java. Datadog's official datadog-api-client-java is a generated wrapper over these same v2 endpoints, and the verify environment is offline and stdlib-only, so java.net.http against the documented reference-tables operations is the credible integration surface; the official client source doubles as the authoritative wire reference.
